title: Incorrect handling of `x` in if blocks (`if (x) else` ) ?
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.54 (git sha1 db72ec3bd, g++ 12.2.0-14+deb12u1 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Hello, 

I think this is possibly a bug as when i tried it with [other simulators](https://edaplayground.com/x/Jrwi) they outputed a 1 on this circuit which is what I would have thought, from running it by hand. 

I am nearly sure that this yosys script is not good, I am sorry I am not yet proficient in yosys scripting. 

```sv
read_verilog -sv <<EOT
module minimal_repro (
    input logic clk,
    output logic out
);
    logic condition;
    logic q1 = 1'bx; // Start with x
    
    always @(posedge clk) begin
        q1 <= 0;
    end

    assign condition = ~q1;

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        if (condition) begin
            // Do nothing: out retains its previous value
        end else begin
            out <= 1'b1;
        end
    end
endmodule
EOT
// here I tried different options prep / proc ; opt / ...
show -prefix minimal_repro

sat -set-def-inputs -set-init-undef -prove out 1 -verify -seq 5 -prove-skip 1
```


### Expected Behavior

I would have expected: 
| cycle | q1 | condition | out |
|---------|-----|---------------|-------|
| 0 | x | x | 1 |
| 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 |
| 2 | 0 | 1 | 1 |
| 3 | 0 | 1 | 1 |
| ... | 0 | 1 | 1 |

Because the `if (condition)` should be evaluated to false as `x` is not truthy, so `out` should be assigned the value `1`.  

### Actual Behavior

At the moment I am not managing to run my script correctly, because it seems to prove that the value can be anything it wants...

if I use `prep` then out is always `x` which I would have thought also wrong and if I use `proc ; opt` I seem to run into #4151 and then the value is always 1 so the proof passes from false to true...

Hopefully it is clear enough, sorry for the bad reproduction steps, Thanks for the work on yosys !
